steps.title: Identify what the task requires
steps.content: The student wants to use one poem to illustrate the sestina form. A correct answer must: (1) select a single poem, (2) accurately state that a sestina has thirty-nine lines with six end words in a set pattern, and (3) use end words that actually belong to the chosen poem.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Hacker employs the sestina, a poetic form with thirty-nine lines and six end words, in both “Forage Sestina” and “Towards Autumn.”': references both poems rather than one, so it does not use a single poem to illustrate the form. ✗
  • Choice B — 'As a sestina, “Towards Autumn” contains thirty-nine lines and six end words—in this case, daughter, friend, bread, mother, lover, and myself—that alternate in a set pattern.': selects one poem, correctly states the form’s features, and uses the exact end words the notes attribute to “Towards Autumn.” ✓
  • Choice C — 'The thirty-nine-line sestina form uses the words daughter, friend, bread, mother, lover, and myself, which are found in the poem “Forage Sestina.”': misattributes these end words — the notes say they belong to “Towards Autumn,” not “Forage Sestina.” ✗
  • Choice D — 'Hacker has used the sestina form multiple times, as in “Towards Autumn,” which contains these six words: words, structure, wire, beam, wall, and room.': misattributes those end words — the notes assign them to “Forage Sestina,” not “Towards Autumn.” ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
